title: Parameterized Access, Authorization, Privacy, and Audit
description: A web service accepts user filters, exposes support data by role, and must resist injection and excessive collection. Learners cumulatively produce a least-privilege parameterized data-access contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Identify code-data boundary failures in dynamic SQL and demonstrate why escaping alone is incomplete.
Bind untrusted values through prepared statements while allow-listing unavoidable identifiers or directions.
Design separate identities, roles, grants, views, and row or column controls for minimum required authority.
